lesson_description: This lesson is about reabsorption in nephron. Students will use computational model to study absorption, selective reabsorption and impact of changing lumen size on absorption in kidney.

steps.title: Watch the video
steps.instruction_text: After watching the video, answer the questions below

steps.title: QUESTION 1.1
steps.instruction_text: Write two questions that you would like to investigate regarding the baby's disease condition.

steps.title: QUESTION 1.2
steps.instruction_text: What are the possible reasons that lead to babies’ excess salt consumption?

steps.title: QUESTION 1.3
steps.instruction_text: What can you conclude from the above video?

steps.title: Watch the video
steps.instruction_text: In this video, researchers at the Indian Institute of Science Education and Research (IISER) Pune talk about how they use computational tools and methods in their work. Scientists and Mathematicians are increasingly using computational tools and methods to understand natural phenomena and solve problems. The thinking required to do so is called computational thinking. In this lesson using a computational model, we will learn computational thinking and reabsorption in the kidney.

steps.title: QUESTION 2.1
steps.instruction_text: Based on what you saw in the video, explain why science students need to develop computational thinking.

steps.title: Explore the model
steps.instruction_text: Explore the model and answer the questions below.

steps.title: QUESTION 3.1
steps.instruction_text: Write any observation/s that you find interesting about the model.

steps.title: QUESTION 3.2
steps.instruction_text: Set the number of molecules to 1000. Vary the chance of absorption, run the model and note down 'molecules absorbed'. Make sure that you 'setup' every time you change a parameter value. Record your results below.

steps.title: QUESTION 3.3
steps.instruction_text: Now set the number of molecules to 500. Again vary the chance of absorption, run the model and note down 'molecules absorbed'. Record your results below.

steps.title: QUESTION 3.4
steps.instruction_text: What can you conclude from your systematic observations regarding absorption in this model?

steps.title: Explore Selective Reabsorption
steps.instruction_text: Explore this model.

steps.title: QUESTION 4.1
steps.instruction_text: In comparison with the previous model, what other factors related to absorption in the kidney can you investigate with this model?

steps.title: QUESTION 4.2
steps.instruction_text: Set the chance of absorption of water to 100% and the chance of salt absorption to 0%. Run your model and note down your observations.

steps.title: QUESTION 4.3
steps.instruction_text: Different parts of a nephron perform different functions. Where in nephron do we observe the above scenario? Explain your answer. [Hint: You can read about Loop of Henle: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Loop_of_Henle]

steps.title: QUESTION 4.4
steps.instruction_text: If you want to investigate the functioning of the ascending loop of Henle, how will you set the model? Mention the parameter values.

steps.title: QUESTION 4.5
steps.instruction_text: Provide justification to the previous answer.

steps.title: Analyze Lumen Size
steps.instruction_text: Explore the model with variable lumen.

steps.title: QUESTION 5.1
steps.instruction_text: Write a question about the impact of lumen size that can be tested with this model. (For example, If we vary 'x', how will it impact 'y'?)

steps.title: QUESTION 5.2
steps.instruction_text: Make a guess about the answer to your question and state it in the form of a hypothesis.

steps.title: QUESTION 5.3
steps.instruction_text: Design an experiment to test your hypothesis. Explain your experimental design.

steps.title: QUESTION 5.4
steps.instruction_text: Collect the experimental data in an excel file. Summarize your collected data below.

steps.title: QUESTION 5.5
steps.instruction_text: Draw conclusion/s from the experimental data.

steps.title: QUESTION 6.1
steps.instruction_text: What could be the advantage/s of using such models for researchers?

steps.title: QUESTION 6.2
steps.instruction_text: What could be the limitation/s of such models?

steps.title: QUESTION 6.3
steps.instruction_text: How can you modify this model of reabsorption to overcome the limitation/s that you mentioned?

steps.title: QUESTION 6.4
steps.instruction_text: Review your hypothesis of the baby’s illness from Page 1. Then, add to or revise your hypothesis for a possible cause of the baby’s illness with reference to the model.

steps.title: QUESTION 6.5
steps.instruction_text: Mention your biggest takeaway from this lesson.
